title: Business Continuity 🛡️
description: Backups and Recovery: Getting back to work.

slides.title: The Golden Rule: 3-2-1 🛡️
slides.content:
### Backups

If a hacker encrypts your files (Ransomware), the only way to get them back for free is from a **Backup**. Follow the **3-2-1 Rule**:

*   **3** copies of your data.
*   **2** different types of media (e.g., Cloud and External Drive).
*   **1** copy stored **offline** (offsite).

slides.title: MTTD and MTTR ⌛
slides.content:
### Measuring Success

*   **MTTD (Mean Time to Detect)**: How long did the hacker live in your system before you found them?
*   **MTTR (Mean Time to Respond)**: How long did it take you to kick them out?

quizData.question: Why should at least one backup be kept offline (not connected to the network)?

options.text: To prevent Ransomware from spreading to and encrypting the backup files

quizData.explanation: If a backup is connected to the network (online), advanced ransomware will find it and encrypt it too.
